description: The Bình Xuyên was an independent military force within the Vietnamese National Army, originally a coalition of gangs and contract laborers formed in the early 1920s in Saigon. It funded itself through organized crime activities such as kidnapping, piracy, and extortion while fighting Communist forces. Led by General Lê Văn Viễn, it was active from 1945 to 1960 and was eventually defeated by the South Vietnamese government.
